title: How Do I Spend My Points?
author: Navees
summary: Churning takes a lot of time and mental energy. So why do it? Here's how I actually spent the points — three redemptions worth way more than I paid.
body_html:
Churning takes up a lot of time and mental energy. So why do it? Here's how I spent a lot of it.

Three redemptions that made the whole game worth it:

1. TAP Portugal — A330

50,000 Chase UR points. Actual seat price: ~$3,000.

2. SriLankan Airlines — A330

80,000 AA miles. Actual seat price: ~$4,000.

3. Qantas — A380

110,000 AA miles. Actual seat price: ~$12,000.

This was probably one of the highlights of any redemption flights I had ever taken. QF First lounge in Melbourne and a pre-flight massage. You do not get that swiping a debit card.

That's the point of all of this. Cash prices I'd never pay, unlocked for a fraction in points.
